source_input.brief: Warm, calm home for a young family that needs a cozy work corner.

pinterest_cluster_labels: Japandi neutral dining set with woven pendant
Minimal plant-forward living room with low wood table

pins.title: Japandi Dining Room with Neutral Decor
pins.description: A Japandi dining room with neutral décor blends Japanese simplicity and Scandinavian warmth. Soft beige, warm wood, and gentle earth tones create a calm, inviting space. Clean lines, natural materials, and minimal furnishings keep the room feeling open and uncluttered, while subtle textures like linen, ceramic, and light wood add quiet elegance and comfort.
pins.cluster_label: Japandi neutral dining set with woven pendant

pins.title: Japandi Living Room: Calm Minimal Decor, Natural Wood & Indoor Plants
pins.description: Calm Japandi living room inspiration with a light neutral sofa, low wooden coffee table, soft textured rug, and plenty of indoor plants for a serene, balanced space. Minimal wall art, natural materials, and clean lines create an airy, soothing aesthetic—perfect for Japanese style interiors, minimalist living room ideas, and warm neutral home decor. Save for Japandi decor and plant-filled living room styling.
pins.cluster_label: Minimal plant-forward living room with low wood table

cluster_summary.label: Japandi neutral dining set with woven pendant
cluster_summary.visual_notes: Round light-oak dining table, slim tapered legs, textured neutral upholstery, oversized woven pendant as focal point, ceramic tableware and hand-thrown vases. Flooring and paneling in pale oak unify the composition—stage with a single sculptural vase, linen place settings, and warm-toned tabletop ceramics.

cluster_summary.label: Low-floor Japanese reading/tea nook
cluster_summary.visual_notes: Intimate corner with floor cushions and low coffee tray, bamboo/shōji screening, layered natural-fiber rugs and woven poufs. Warm paper/lantern lighting creates deep amber glow—use soft cushions, round woven floor mats, low tray table and minimal potted greenery to recreate cozy scale.

cluster_summary.label: Minimal plant-forward living room with low wood table
cluster_summary.visual_notes: Low, neutral sofa on wooden base, organic-shaped low coffee table, vertical wood slat accent and sparse framed art. Emphasize neutral textiles, textured rug, and multiple medium potted plants for vertical green accents—keep decor minimal: 2–3 dark ceramic vessels and one sculptural plant grouping.

rooms.name: Nest
rooms.windows: bay window / large glazed opening toward relax area

rooms.name: Dream
rooms.windows: single exterior window on top wall

rooms.name: Taste
rooms.windows: small window/vent above counter (adjacent exterior wall)

rooms.name: Dine
rooms.windows: bay-style multi-pane window on exterior corner

vibe_report.summary: A calm, warm Japandi interior that balances Japanese minimal restraint with Scandinavian warmth. Prioritize low-profile furniture, natural materials (light oak, walnut, jute), layered textures, and a single cozy work corner for a young family. Keep decoration minimal and purposeful: 2–3 ceramics, one sculptural plant cluster, and a woven pendant as focal punctuation above the dining table.
vibe_report.palette_rationale: Warm neutrals anchored by pale sand and light oak (#EDE6DE, #D2BFA3) with mid-tone walnut accents (#B58E61) and restrained deep anchors (#2F3230). This palette keeps the rooms feeling bright and calm while adding tactile warmth for family use.
vibe_report.lighting_mood: Soft ambient base light with directional window pulls. Use warm practicals (paper/linen table lamps, a woven pendant) to create layered pockets of amber in evenings. Daytime scenes rely on visible exterior windows as primary directional light sources—no invented light sources.
textures: flatwoven jute rug with subtle chevron
furniture_language: slim tapered legs on tables and seating
natural, tactile finishes over glossy surfaces
styling_rules: limit tabletop decor to 2–3 objects (ceramic vase, bowl, small tray)
keep art minimal: single narrow frame or vertical slat detail per wall
anchor seating areas with one large woven rug, neutral color
prioritize family durability: matte finishes, rounded corners
work corner must be low-visual-weight and tuckable (no bulky office chair)
avoid: large, high-backed sofas that break sightlines

room_guidance.headline: Low, plant-forward family living with tucked work corner
room_guidance.guidance: Compose a low 3-seater sofa facing the bay window with a low walnut coffee table on the handwoven jute rug. Add a compact standing_desk in a corner near the window for natural light and to create a visible but unobtrusive work nook. Include 2–3 medium potted plants for vertical interest.
must_include: low-profile 3-seater sofa — pale oat linen upholstery, dark walnut tapered legs, low 18" seat height, chaise-free symmetrical form
large woven area rug — handwoven jute in warm sand (#EDE6DE), 8x10, flatweave with subtle chevron texture
organic low coffee table — oval solid walnut coffee table, matte oil finish, rounded edges, 36in x 20in, low 12" height
compact standing desk — warm ash veneer top, matte black steel legs, 48" width, integrated cable channel, minimal apron
must_avoid: bright throw pillows or heavy patterned textiles

room_guidance.headline: Serene, low-profile bedroom with tactile layering
room_guidance.guidance: Keep the bed low and centered to window wall; use pale linens and a single walnut bedside table. A narrow open bookshelf can act as visual storage near the shared wall if needed. Maintain calm sightlines toward the living room—no bold color interruptions.
must_include: tall open bookshelf — dark walnut open-shelf with 5 tiers, slim vertical profile, matte finish
soft linen bedding in pale oat (#EDE6DE)
must_avoid: high-contrast bedding or overly ornate headboards

room_guidance.headline: Compact kitchen with warm materials and visible, calm surfaces
room_guidance.guidance: Keep counters decluttered, use warm oak open shelving and matte black fixtures. Small practical pendant above sink only if visible in frame. Ensure visual connection to dining and living—materials must match adjacent surfaces.
must_include: round light-oak dining table — solid light oak, 48-inch diameter, slim tapered legs, natural oiled finish
matte black hardware accents

room_guidance.headline: Tactile dining nook with woven pendant as focal point
room_guidance.guidance: Place the round light-oak dining table beneath the oversized woven pendant and stage with a single sculptural ceramic vase and linen place settings. Keep chairs slim and upholstered in textured neutral fabric.
must_include: round light-oak dining table — solid light oak, 48-inch diameter, slim tapered legs, natural oiled finish
oversized woven pendant — oversized circular woven rattan pendant, natural straw color, 24-inch diameter, suspended matte black canopy

room_guidance.headline: Minimal, spa-like bath with ceramic and wood warmth
room_guidance.guidance: Use matte ceramic tiles, a walnut vanity, and a single sculptural ceramic soap dish. Keep the frosted window as the primary light source; introduce a warm sconce near vanity for evening shots.
must_include: walnut vanity — low-profile walnut vanity with matte oil finish and integrated basin

warnings: Kitchen and bathroom dimensions were estimated — verify final staging scale on-site.
Open-plan sightlines require strict furniture color/material consistency to avoid visible mismatches between living, dining, and kitchen.

creative_spec.overall_mood: Quiet, warm, family-ready Japandi — tactile surfaces, low silhouettes, and a single cozy work corner.
global_style_notes: Preserve architecture and sightlines; do not add furniture that would break the open-plan flow.
Use one consistent wood tone palette (light oak + walnut accents) across adjacent rooms.
Night and day edits must derive light only from visible windows and practical fixtures.
creative_spec.negative_prompt: blur, distort, low quality, warped architecture, flicker, unstable camera, unrealistic lighting, muddy shadows, layout drift, no color inconsistency between rooms, no mismatched furniture across sightlines

caption_context.property_brief: Warm, calm home for a young family that needs a cozy work corner.
caption_context.aesthetic_summary: A calm, warm Japandi interior that balances Japanese minimal restraint with Scandinavian warmth. Prioritize low-profile furniture, natural materials (light oak, walnut, jute), layered textures, and a single cozy work corner for a young family. Keep decoration minimal and purposeful: 2–3 ceramics, one sculptural plant cluster, and a woven pendant as focal punctuation above the dining table.

annotations.label: Mood
annotations.value: Quiet, warm, family-ready Japandi — tactile surfaces, low silhouettes, and a single cozy work corner.

warnings.message: Double-check on-site scale for kitchen and bathroom before final prop purchase—measurements were estimated by ML parser.
Because multiple rooms share sightlines, ensure set dressing and materials are physically consistent across adjacent rooms (same oak tone, same linen color).
For AutoHDR video flow: prefer raw-photo -> image pass -> video for rooms with flat light; skip image pass only if input photos already have strong directional light and clean verticals.
Kitchen and bathroom dimensions were estimated — verify final staging scale on-site.
Open-plan sightlines require strict furniture color/material consistency to avoid visible mismatches between living, dining, and kitchen.
